input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually

tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - MTP returns exit code 8 when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as success.

Let me look at their project files to understand the setup, and also invoke the relevant skill.

 ConditionalTests.cs
 TestProject.csproj

data.content:
This is a known MTP behavioral difference. **Exit code 8 means "no tests discovered."** VSTest returned 0 for that case; MTP treats it as an error by default.

The fix is a single MSBuild property:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP), exit code 8 means "no tests were found." When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, the entire test class is compiled out, resulting in zero tests, and MTP returns exit code 8 (unlike VSTest which returned 0).

The fix is to set the `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` property to `0` in the project file. This MSBuild property tells MTP to return exit code 0 when no tests are discovered instead of exit code 8.

Let me apply the fix.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-572b4aaa4e004ba0ae343bef74829375/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.content: Now let me verify it builds and returns exit code 0 with zero tests:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Build and run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Build and run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS

arguments.description: Build and run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-572b4aaa4e004ba0ae343bef74829375 && dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1; echo \"EXIT CODE: $?\"", "description": "Build and run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Build and run tests without INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS

data.content: Exit code is now **0** with zero tests discovered. Let me also confirm it still works correctly when tests *are* present:

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-572b4aaa4e004ba0ae343bef74829375 && dotnet test -p:DefineConstants=INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS --no-restore 2>&1; echo \"EXIT CODE: $?\"", "description": "Run tests with INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS defined", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content: Both scenarios pass. Here's the final state of the project file:

data.content:
**Summary:** Added `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` to the project file.

MTP exit code 8 specifically means "no tests discovered" — it's a safety net to catch misconfigured test assemblies. Since your zero-test case is intentional (conditional compilation), setting this property to `0` restores the VSTest behavior. If you have multiple test projects, you can put this in a shared `Directory.Build.props` instead.
